title: Eremanthus sphaerocephalus
taxonomy: Eremanthus sphaerocephalus is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is one of the largest and most widespread families of flowering plants. Within the Asteraceae family, Eremanthus sphaerocephalus belongs to the subfamily Asteroideae, which comprises a large group of flowering plants that have a similar flower structure. intro: Eremanthus sphaerocephalus, also known as the Brazilian buttonbush, is a shrubby plant native to the subtropical regions of South America, characterized by its small, round flower heads and attractive foliage. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and protect against cell damage.
